title: Penetration forming apparatus and method of use
abstract: A penetration former comprising an assembly comprising a rigid frame having an open upper end and an opposite open lower end is disclosed, the frame defining an internal passage extending from the upper end to the lower end and being adapted for receiving a weight-bearing barrier, which, when operatively located in relation to the frame, blocks the passage. In use, the frame is attached to a deck former sheet, such as plywood. Concrete or other deck-forming material is poured on to the deck former sheet and around the frame, so that the passage is left open. The former sheet is removed, leaving the frame to define a penetration. A closure sheet is placed over the penetration passage pending installation of a conduit.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  The claims defining the invention are: 1. A penetration former comprising a rigid frame having an open upper end and an opposite open lower end, the frame defining an internal passage extending from said upper end to said lower end, the frame being adapted for receiving a weight- bearing barrier, which, when operatively located in relation to the frame, blocks the passage against entry. 2. A former according to claim 1 , wherein the frame is adapted for fixing to a temporary deck. 3. A former according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the barrier is securable to the frame when operatively located to block the passage. 4. A former according to claim 3, wherein the barrier comprises a rigid panel. 5. A former according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the frame defines a retaining wall in first and second attachable parts. 6. A former according to claim 5 wherein the first and second attachable parts comprise a plurality of separably connectable side sections and corner elements. 7. A former according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the frame comprises a rim at the upper end and a ledge inward of and below the rim, the ledge being configured for operatively supporting the barrier. 8. A former according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the lower end comprises a flange. 9. A former according to any one of the preceding claims, adapted for removal and reuse after the slab has formed. 10. A former according to claim 9, wherein the barrier is operatively locatable in the penetration after removal of the former. 1 1. A method of forming a blockable penetration in an edifice, the method comprising the steps of: providing a temporary deck; locating a former comprising a rigid frame on the deck, the frame defining a passage therethrough from an upper to an opposite lower end; operatively locating a weight-bearing barrier to block the passage; disposing concrete on the temporary deck to surround the frame; and allowing the concrete to set to form a concrete slab over the temporary deck. 12. A method according to claim 1 1 , including the step of securing the former to the temporary deck. 13. A method according to claim 1 1 or claim 12, including the step of removing the temporary deck after the concrete slab has set, thereby to allow access to the passage. 14. A method according to any one of claims 1 1 to 13 comprising the step of securing the barrier within the former. 15. A method according to claim 14, wherein the former comprises an inner ledge on which the barrier rests when in operative orientation. 16. A method according to any one of claims 1 1 to 15 comprising removing the former after the concrete slab has set. 17. A method according to claim 17, comprising locating the barrier in the penetration vacated by the former. 18. A method according to any one of claims 16 or 17 comprising making the removed former available for reuse in forming another penetration. 19. A method according to any one of claims 16 to 18 comprising the step of applying a cover over the barrier. 20. A method according to claim 19, comprising securing the cover to the slab. 21. A penetration forming and blocking kit comprising a former defining a penetration passage and a barrier in panel form, the former comprising a frame adapted to receive and support the barrier in operative, passage-blocking orientation. 22. A kit according to claim 21 wherein the former comprises an upper wall part and a lower wall part adapted to fit one within the other. 23. A kit according to claim 22 wherein the wall parts comprise separate connectable corner elements and side sections. 24. A kit according to claim 23 wherein the sections and elements are adapted to have a plain end and an opposite recessed end, the recessed end being adapted to connectably receive a plain end of an adjacent element or section. 25. A kit according to claim 24 wherein the sections and elements are connectable to provide a continuous surface on the wall parts. 26. A kit according to claim 25 wherein the continuous surface of the upper wall part is adapted to abut the continuous surface of the lower wall part. 27. A kit according to claim 26 wherein the upper and lower parts are adapted for mutual connection. 28. A kit according to claim 27 wherein the upper and lower wall parts are adapted by having engageable formations. 29. A kit according to claim 28 wherein the formations include a rib which lies generally horizontal between said abutting continuous surfaces. 30. A kit according to any one of claims 22 to 29, wherein the upper wall part comprises an inner ledge adapted to support the barrier. 31. A kit according to any one of claims 21 to 30, including fastenings for fastening the former to a supporting temporary deck prior to casting of a concrete slab on the deck. 32. A kit according to any one of claims 21 to 31 , including a cover plate, adapted for operatively positioning over the barrier pending installation of a service conduit in the passage. 33. A kit according to claim 32 including fastenings for securing the cover to a concrete slab in which a penetration is to be formed. 34. A kit according to any one of claims 21 to 33, wherein the frame has open upper and lower ends connected by a passage suitable for receiving services conduits to pass therethrough in use.
